title: Do not use 'AI-powered' as a default feature modifier
summary: AI-generated copy appends 'AI-powered' to every feature description regardless of whether AI is the primary mechanism or a meaningful differentiator.
instruction: Use 'AI-powered' only when AI/ML is the core mechanism and meaningful differentiator of the feature. Describe what the feature actually does rather than slapping 'AI' on it.
rationale: When every feature is 'AI-powered', the term loses all differentiation. Users cannot distinguish genuinely AI-driven capabilities from conventional features. Describing the actual function ('Summarizes 50-page documents in 8 seconds') is more informative and honest.

violations: 'AI-powered analytics dashboard' where the analytics are standard aggregations and SQL queries.
'AI-powered email responses' for a template-based reply system with no ML component.
controls: 'AI-powered background removal' using a real computer vision model — AI is the core mechanism and differentiator.
'AI-powered translation in 95 languages' where the AI model is the primary technology.
borderline: A feature description for an AI company where 'AI-powered' is part of the brand promise and the entire product IS AI — should still describe the specific mechanism.

exceptions.description: Products whose core technology IS AI (e.g., OpenAI, Anthropic) where 'AI-powered' is a category descriptor, not a feature modifier. Academic or technical documentation specifying the ML approach.
examples: API documentation for an AI model endpoint
Product description for an AI-first company

detector.signalDescription: Match 'AI-powered' or 'AI driven' as a feature modifier in marketing copy. Check if the surrounding description explains the AI mechanism or just uses the phrase decoratively.
limitations: Cannot determine whether AI is genuinely the core mechanism without product understanding.
Some features legitimately use AI as the primary differentiator.

scenarios: An AI-first company where every feature is genuinely AI-driven.
Technical documentation accurately describing the AI component.
mitigations: Flag only marketing copy, not technical docs. Check for specificity in the 50 characters following 'AI-powered'.

copyPattern.replacementStrategy: Replace 'AI-powered [feature]' with a specific description of what the feature does and how it achieves it. Reserve 'AI' for features where it's the genuine differentiator.

fixturePlan.description: A SaaS page listing 'AI-powered analytics, AI-powered reports, AI-powered dashboards' with no mechanism description.
Same page describing 'Real-time analytics' and 'Automated report generation' without the AI prefix, with one genuinely AI feature described specifically.
An AI API company's landing page where 'AI-powered' describes the platform category.
